current_focus.name: Algebraic Surface Hardening
current_focus.rationale: The flagship QR and SPD workflow passes are strong enough that the next substrate gap is transpose-heavy algebra, not another broad expansion of algorithm families. The current src_id transpose shortcut fixes the GPU re-upload problem, but the remaining work is to replace it with a proper structural view and expose full GEMM control explicitly.
deferred_until_after_current_focus: New decomposition families beyond the current SVD/QR/Cholesky/block-Lanczos set

milestones.name: Bootstrap Core Package
deliverables: Create amatrix package skeleton
Define adgeMatrix and adgCMatrix classes
Add constructors and coercion helpers
Add backend registry and policy interfaces
Implement CPU backend stubs and host realization layer
exit_criteria: Package loads on macOS with no accelerated backend installed
Core objects validate as Matrix-compatible subclasses
All public methods route through a single backend dispatch layer

milestones.name: CPU Semantic Baseline
deliverables: Implement dense core operations and decompositions on the CPU reference path
Add serialization and deserialization behavior
Document fallback semantics
exit_criteria: CPU-only outputs match Matrix/base semantics for covered operations
Fallback paths are deterministic and tested
Serialization does not persist stale backend state

milestones.name: Conformance Harness
deliverables: Randomized dense conformance tests
Randomized sparse conformance tests
Fixture coverage for metadata, dimnames, and edge values
Backend parity and integration scaffolding
exit_criteria: Every covered method has a CPU-reference conformance test
The suite runs in CPU-only CI
Optional backend implementations plug into the same parity harness

milestones.name: Apple Dense Backend
deliverables: Create amatrix.mlx package
Implement dense MLX kernels for the supported surface
Add backend cache lifecycle management
Benchmark representative dense workloads on Apple Silicon
exit_criteria: MLX-backed methods pass backend parity tests under the supported contract
Unsupported paths realize host state and complete correctly
At least one benchmark class shows a meaningful speedup versus CPU baseline

milestones.name: Portable Dense Backend
deliverables: Create amatrix.arrayfire package
Implement dense ArrayFire kernels for the backend contract
Add backend-selection policy tests
exit_criteria: ArrayFire backend passes the covered dense parity suite
No public API changes are required in amatrix
ArrayFire remains an optional install-time dependency

milestones.name: Product Hardening And Contract Freeze
deliverables: Make am_rsvd precision-aware so backend dispatch is fast-only
Keep the broad backend-integration and conformance slice green under the strict-by-default contract
Sync PRD, README, and roadmap docs to the implementation that actually ships
Freeze the public mode/backend story around explicit backend opt-in
exit_criteria: Broad backend-integration, conformance, and cross-backend-conformance slices pass on the supported machine
Strict precision never dispatches float32-only backend decomposition paths
README and docs no longer promise balanced auto-GPU routing
The current flagship workflow and the next frontier are documented consistently

milestones.name: Flagship Workflow Validation And Cold-Path Cost Reduction
deliverables: Add broader parity and benchmark coverage for MLX GPU Cholesky plus batched triangular solve
Reduce compact MLX QR cold factor-build cost and measure its end-to-end impact on many_lm
Publish updated flagship benchmark notes for QR and SPD solve workflows
exit_criteria: MLX chol_factor and chol_solve paths have focused tests and benchmark evidence
Compact QR cold-build cost is no longer the dominant unexplained overhead in the flagship workflow
Flagship docs point to benchmark-backed QR and Cholesky adoption guidance

milestones.name: Algebraic Surface Hardening
deliverables: Replace the current src_id-linked transpose shortcut with a proper aTransposeView or aMatrixView design
Preserve fast crossprod/tcrossprod routing for t(A) %*% B and A %*% t(B) while removing the remaining host transpose cost
Promote internal .am_gemm to a documented public am_gemm() surface for explicit alpha/beta/accumulator control
Align contract, PRD, and migration docs around structural views for common cases and explicit kernels for full control
exit_criteria: Docs and issue tracker agree that structural views are allowed while full GEMM control stays explicit
The transpose-view implementation path preserves distinct cache and residency identity from the source matrix
t(t(A)) has a clean collapse rule without redundant host transposition

milestones.name: Advanced Decomposition Frontier
deliverables: Tighten and decide promotion criteria for the first block Lanczos prototype now that the initial MLX benchmark wins are landed
Decide whether ArrayFire rsvd quality and performance are product-ready or should remain experimental
Add deterministic or better-bounded testing strategy for stochastic backend decomposition paths where needed
exit_criteria: Block Lanczos has a benchmark-backed case for beating the current am_irlba compatibility path and at least one CPU crossover point, and the current approximation envelope is documented honestly
ArrayFire decomposition status is documented honestly
Backend-local stochastic decomposition tests are stable enough for maintenance
